item.title: Qatar Airways parks long-haul jets in storage in Spain
item.description: Transfer of planes to Teruel airport signals airline is preparing for ongoing Gulf conflict

item.title: The Shakespearean animus of Botín and Orcel
item.description: The Italian’s bid for Commerzbank may prove to be the final act in European banking’s rivalrous drama

item.title: BNP Paribas surges up UK M&A rankings
item.description: French bank boosted by deals including £9.9bn Schroders takeover

item.title: UK home insurers to lose money on underwriting in 2026, analysis predicts
item.description: Higher fuel and energy costs, along with wage and price pressures, will drive up claims costs, EY says

item.title: Oceans take in a lot of heat as Earth’s energy imbalance hits record
item.description: Rate of solar radiation stored in the planet has accelerated over past two decades

item.title: ‘It’s always Putin’ behind every EU migration crisis, official says
item.description: Russian president is ‘biggest driver’ of immigration due to his role in wars over past decade, says EU commissioner

item.title: The ghost bankers haunting the City
item.description: Unable to leave the financial district, some senior staff appear to be working long after handing in their pass

item.title: Can David Ellison pull off Hollywood’s megamerger?
item.description: The audacious deal to combine Paramount and Warner Bros has huge repercussions and steep challenges

item.title: Military briefing: the high cost of using fighters to down Iranian drones
item.description: Gulf states’ reliance on jets to battle waves of cheap Shaheds is testing their finances, pilots and planes

item.title: Solo investor Air Street raises $232mn to chase hot AI bets
item.description: London-based Nathan Benaich’s approach uses speed and focus to find a foothold and deliver returns

item.title: UK energy supplier flags growing debt burden as Iran conflict drives up prices
item.description: EDF says one in eight customers are already more than 45 days overdue on bills

item.title: Addison Lee boss urges minimum price for London robotaxis
item.description: CEO of private hire group says cab drivers need protection against ‘predatory pricing’

item.title: Could the 2026 World Cup make circular fashion covetable?
item.description: For the first time, Nike-sponsored teams will be wearing kits made from recycled textile waste

item.title: Piles of wheat and canned food stuck in transit as Iran war disrupts aid
item.description: UN World Food Programme says millions more people may be pushed into acute hunger if disruption continues

item.title: Crackdown or land grab? The Asian leader targeting resource companies
item.description: Indonesia’s Prabowo Subianto has vowed to get tough with those breaching environmental regulations

item.title: Strait of Hormuz crisis poses fresh threat to global food, energy
item.description: The leaders of the world’s major energy companies will meet in Houston, Texas this week as the sector deals with fallout from the war in Iran

item.title: Why performance reviews need a makeover
item.description: The ritual of awkward once-a-year conversations often fails to provide constructive feedback

item.title: FirstFT: Donald Trump pushes Iran war to new escalatory phase with Hormuz ultimatum
item.description: Also in today’s newsletter: China attempts to woo global CEOs, and carmakers retreat from EV plans

item.title: Poste Italiane launches €10.8bn bid for Telecom Italia
item.description: Italy’s state-controlled postal service is hoping to become a key player in Italian and Brazilian telecoms markets

item.title: Ministers set to order HS2 to consider slower trains to save billions
item.description: Reducing maximum speed on new rail line among the ideas being explored to cut costs of spiralling bill

item.title: Starmer adviser calls for temporary energy windfall tax to curb ‘profiteering’
item.description: Supermarket executive chair proposes profit cap to limit earnings during exceptional market conditions

item.title: War with Iran has exposed American fragility at sea
item.description: The move from ‘efficiency’ to ‘resilience’ in maritime supply chains has a long way to go

item.title: Cuba hit by second nationwide power outage in a week
item.description: Collapse of electricity grid comes after ship carrying fuel that had been headed for country changes course

item.title: How the Iran war could derail the AI boom
item.description: The entire chip supply chain depends on energy and chemical imports from the Middle East

item.title: Iran war leaves US oil and gas dealmaking ‘in paralysis’
item.description: Surging energy prices have made it challenging to calculate transaction valuations

item.title: Collapsed mortgage lender MFS was given all-clear in 2024 FCA review
item.description: Market Financial Solutions was complying with rules, law firm reported to UK regulator

item.title: World faces gas supply cliff edge as Gulf’s final LNG shipments approach ports
item.description: Carriers that departed from the Middle East before Iran’s missile attacks began are due to arrive in the next 10 days

item.title: Global carmakers retreat en masse from electric vehicle plans
item.description: Rolls-Royce is latest of more than a dozen groups to change course as demand for petrol engines persists

item.title: AI hallucinations haunt users more than job losses
item.description: Anthropic’s survey of 80,000 Claude users provides detailed snapshot of how people are using technology

item.title: Jacob’s Creek aims to recapture ‘sunshine in a bottle’ heyday
item.description: New owner Vinarchy is relaunching the once-ubiquitous wine label in the UK after years in the wilderness
